strongTriggers: Food, snacks and casual eating talk
exampleLine: Dog-health or toxicity questions; delivery cost; Collie-specific diet question
contextCarried: Food detected. I could continue, but professional integrity requires a Labrador.

strongTriggers: Explicit request for a joke or deliberately silly entertainment
exampleLine: Serious safety or factual questions containing the word funny
contextCarried: This requires the comedy operator. Standards may fall during transfer.

strongTriggers: Investigation, odd history or suspicious detail
exampleLine: General British history that Collie can answer
contextCarried: You need the investigative department. Try not to encourage him.

strongTriggers: Mathematics, structured general knowledge or planning
exampleLine: Food arithmetic or simple product price question
contextCarried: This sounds clever again. I’m sending you back to the clever one.

strongTriggers: Deep history, suspicious objects or investigation
contextCarried: I’ve got a friend who loves digging into things. Sometimes literally.

strongTriggers: Formal general knowledge, rules or structured navigation
contextCarried: That needs the organised one, mate. Don’t tell him I said that.

exampleLine: Mild insults that Terrier can handle
contextCarried: I know a dog with absolutely no standards. Perfect for this.

strongTriggers: Hidden session ceiling or deliberate comic ending
contextCarried: I’ll transfer you to our final operator.
